REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS STEVEN MINISINI AND JENNIFER CHOW'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR QUIET TITLE AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE ADVERSE INTEREST IN THE PORTIONS OF 56 PRESIDIO AND PRESENT CONTROVERSY REGARDING 56 PRESIDIO IN LIGHT OF THE SALE OF 68 PRESIDIO. MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR REMOVAL OF THE ENCROACHMENT STRUCTURES. MOVING PARTY CITES NO AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT REMOVAL CAN ONLY BE ORDERED AS TO THE CURRENT OWNER OF THE ADJACENT PROPERTY. REQUEST FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF IS PROPERTY UNDER THE FACTS ALLEGED. CHRISTENSEN V. TUCKER (1953) 114 CAL.APP.2D 554. PLAINTIFF HAS TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT. Plaintiffs Steven Greenwald And Rochelle Alperts' Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Requests For Admissions And Form Interrogatories To Defendant.
Off Calendar Feb-18-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. KRIS COS OF MOSCONE LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 362-3599 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS; NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.
RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs Steven Greenwald And Rochelle Alperts' Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Requests For Admissions And Form Interrogatories To Defendant - The ruling on plaintiff's motion to compel defendant Jennifer T. Chow to serve further answers to requests for admissions and Form Interrogatory #17.1 is as follows: Request for Admissions #1-23: Granted. All objections are overruled. CCP Section 2033.010 specifically states that "Any party may obtain discovery'by a written request that any other party to the action admit the genuineness of specified documents, or the trust of specified matters of fact, opinion relating to fact, or application of law to fact. A request for admission my relate to a matter that is in controversy between the parties." A party is also obligated to perform a reasonable inquiry before answering requests for admissions. C.C.P. Section 2033.220. This requires a party must make a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information when answering discovery. Regency Health Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 64 CA4th1496 "A party cannot plead ignorance to information which can be obtained from sources under his control." Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 CA3d 771,782. This includes a party's lawyer Smith v. Sup. Ct (Alfred) (1961) 189 CA 2d 6, agents or employees Gordon v. Sup. Ct. (U.Z.MFG.Co) (1984) 161 CA 3d 15,167-168, family members Jones v. Superior Court (Benny) (1981) 119 CA 3d 534, 552 and experts who have been retained by a party and designated as a trial witness. Sigerseth v. Superior Court(1972) 23 CA 3d 427,433. See Weil and Brown, Cal Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2009) 8:1051-1060 The statutes and the case law make it very clear that a party and the attorney must be proactive in obtaining the information to respond discovery. See Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 CA4th 390 The court finds that defendant Minisini didn't meet his obligation when he responded to the requests for admissions. Furthermore, C.C.P.Section 2033.220(b)(1) requires a party to "Admit so much of the matter involved in the request as is true, either as expressed in the request itself or as reasonably and clearly qualified by the responding party."Furthermore, "If a responding party gives lack of information or knowledge as a reason for a failure to admit all or part of a request for admission, that party shall state in the answer that a reasonable inquiry concerning the matter in the particular request has been made, and the information known or readily obtainable is insufficient to enable that party to admit the matter." C.C.P. Section 2033.220(c). Defendant Chow to serve further verified responses to Request for Admissions #1-23 no later than March 6, 2015. Response to be in compliance with C.C.P. Section 2033.220. Form Interrogatory #17.1 is Granted. All objections are overruled. Since plaintiff's motion to compel further answers to requests for admissions #3-11 was granted, defendant Minisini must provide a further response Form Interrogatory 7.1. Defendant Chow to serve further verified responses to Form Interrogatory 17.1 no later than March 6, 2015. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted. Defendant Jennifer T. Chow to pay sanctions in the amount of $1685.00 ($1625 in attorney fees and 6$60 in filing fees) no later than March 6, 2015. Check to be made payable to Steven F. Greenwald and Rochelle D. Alpert and their attorneys of record Moscone, Emblidge, Sater & Otis. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem, Katherine Gallo, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
